prompt: I have a JavaFX controller that extends `BaseController` from the framework. My override of `initialize()` does work that doesn't make sense in tests — it touches the live UI, depends on framework lifecycle, and would throw if called in any other context. The base class's contract is being broken in my override. Is there a clean SOLID-aligned way to deal with this?

assertions.text: Names the Humble Object pattern explicitly
Prescribes a two-class split: thin shell doing framework-required work + plain object with the logic
Describes the shell as legitimately constrained (hard to test, may throw on inapplicable operations) — the LSP violation is quarantined there
Describes the plain object as testable and framework-free
Generalizes the pattern: applies whenever inheritance you don't control forces a contract break
